name: Kuyavian-Pomeranian Voivodeship (Poland)

colors.symbolism: from the arms of Kuyavia and Pomerelia

description: Three horizontal stripes of red, white, and black, with the white stripe twice the width of the others.
history: Adopted by the Kuyavian-Pomeranian Voivodeship after its creation in 1999, combining the heraldic colours of the historic Kuyavian and Pomerelian regions.

features.symbolism: Combines colours from the historic flags of Kuyavia (red and white) and Pomerelia (black and white), the two regions joined to form the voivodeship.
